_unit_notes.prefill_tps: 1/slope of (ttft vs input tokens); honest prefill speed
_unit_notes.decode_tps: 1/slope of (wall vs output tokens); honest decode speed
_unit_notes.warm_ttft_s: median ttft on smallest (256-tok) input; short output flushes promptly

latency.methodology: Cold = first streamed call before warmup. Prefill = median ttft over 4 runs per input size [256, 512, 1024, 2048, 3072], fit linearly (ttft vs input). Decode = total wall over 4 runs x counts [120, 250, 450], fit linearly (wall vs output tokens) — no-stream because long outputs flush at completion, making ttft~=wall. Warm ttft from 256-tok runs (short output). Greedy throughout (--greedy) for reproducibility.

context.methodology: Ceiling = largest input where the model retrieves a final needle (binary search, guardrail-safe filler, refusal vs context-error distinguished). Recall = 3 facts at 30/60/90% of docs of [512, 1024, 2048, 3000] tokens, queried separately; recall_fraction = correct/3. Greedy. Ceiling=can-FIT; recall=can-RETRIEVE.

honest_summary.interpretation: Differential en0 outbound bytes = inference-window minus idle-window. A small/zero excess with NO model-related process in nettop is evidence (not proof) of on-device privacy. Proving offline requires disabling the network (manual wifi test).

privacy.methodology: netstat -ib en0 Obytes delta + nettop -P -x -J bytes_in,bytes_out -t external -L 45 during a ~24s greedy generation vs equal idle. en0 bytes are total-interface (background services contribute); only the differential excess is attributable to the activity window.
